prompt: Create a .NET 9 inventory reservation system with InventoryItem aggregate, Reservation entity, business rules (no over-reserve, 15min expiry, partial reservations, confirm/cancel lifecycle, optimistic concurrency), Result<T>, value objects, xUnit tests.
assertions: Value objects (ProductId, Quantity) are separate classes — not raw Guid/int
InventoryItem is the aggregate root — Reservations are accessed through it, not independently
Private setters on all entities — no public property setters
Private constructors with static factory methods returning Result<T>
Result<T> used for operations that can fail — no exceptions for business rules
Cannot reserve more than available quantity
Partial reservations supported — returns shortfall information
Confirming a reservation permanently decreases available stock
Cancelling/expiring a reservation returns stock to available
Optimistic concurrency via version field that increments on mutations
Tests cover normal flow, edge cases, and concurrency scenarios
Domain events raised for significant state changes
